data.title: Ado-Ekiti to Lagos Storm Multi-Vehicle Incident Postmortem
data.content: Incident summary: During severe rain and low visibility on Ekiti-Lagos corridor, a large trailer, a nine-seater bus, and multiple passenger vehicles were involved in a chain collision. Contributing factors included speed mismatch, reduced traction, delayed braking distance, and lane uncertainty under heavy rain. Immediate recommendations: dynamic speed reduction protocol, hazard-light spacing discipline, and mandatory no-overtake rule under red rain conditions.

data.title: Heavy Rain Driving Decision Playbook
data.content: Decision protocol for severe rain: If visibility under 80 meters or wiper max speed still insufficient, reduce speed by at least 40 percent from dry-road baseline. If hydroplaning risk indicators appear (steering lightness, wheel-slip, standing water), disengage cruise behavior, avoid hard braking, and increase following distance to 6-8 seconds. Trigger safe-stop recommendation when thunderstorm intensity sustains beyond 10 minutes with persistent poor visibility.

data.title: Roadside Incident First 10 Minutes SOP
data.content: Within first 10 minutes: 1) Scene safety first, do not add secondary victims. 2) Alert emergency services and nearest traffic authority. 3) Establish hazard perimeter with safe standoff. 4) Prioritize trapped occupants and visible severe bleeding. 5) Avoid moving victims with spinal risk unless immediate fire/flood danger exists. 6) Record lane and weather context for responders.

data.title: Transport Fleet Weather Escalation SOP
data.content: Escalation matrix: Green weather means normal operations. Amber weather enforces 20 percent speed reduction and no risky overtaking. Red weather enforces 40 percent speed reduction, dispatch advisories every 10 minutes, and optional controlled stop at approved safe bays. Black weather (extreme storm cells, near-zero visibility, flooding) mandates immediate halt at nearest safe location and incident status broadcast.

data.title: Driver Voice Assistant Guidance Standard
data.content: In-cabin AI guidance must be concise, non-distracting, and action-prioritized. During acute hazard periods, limit output to top 2 immediate actions and one confirmation question. Avoid long explanations while vehicle is in active control state. Provide uncertainty explicitly and request safe pull-over for detailed guidance when risk is high.

data.title: Storm Commute Pre-Departure Checklist
data.content: Pre-departure checks: tire tread, brake response, wiper condition, demister function, headlamp clarity, hazard lamp function, emergency kit availability, and route weather watch. If two or more critical checks fail, delay trip or switch transport mode.

data.title: Sandwiched Vehicle Rescue Coordination Playbook
data.content: For under-trailer entrapment: secure scene and traffic flow first, prevent fuel ignition sources, coordinate extraction with trained rescue units, and communicate casualty count with confidence range. Civilians should not attempt structural lifting without rescue command approval due to crush instability.

data.title: Public Communication Guidance During Highway Crashes
data.content: Public updates should prioritize route diversion, emergency lane preservation, and misinformation reduction. Use verified status only. Do not publish unconfirmed casualty identities.
